me.enable2faDesc: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app or enter the key manually.
me.2faCodeDesc: Enter the code from your authenticator app.
me.disable2faDesc: Enter your password to disable Two Factor Authentication.

setup.welcome: Welcome to your first setup of PhobosWG
setup.welcomeDesc: You have found the easiest way to deploy a resilient WireGuard
setup.existingSetup: Do you have an existing setup?
setup.createAdminDesc: Please first enter an admin username and a strong secure password. This information will be used to log in to your administration panel.
setup.setupConfigDesc: Please enter the public hostname or address. It is used for display and TLS; clients connect via the obfuscator port.
setup.setupMigrationDesc: Please provide the backup file if you want to migrate your data from your previous wg-easy version to your new setup.
setup.hostDesc: Public hostname or IP (e.g. for the web UI and certificates)

tls.title: HTTPS / TLS Certificate
tls.desc: Configure a TLS certificate to enable secure HTTPS access. Without HTTPS, login is blocked by default.
tls.selfSignedDesc: Generated instantly for your host. Browser will show a security warning — click Advanced → Proceed.
tls.selfSignedHstsWarning: If your browser blocks the page with an HSTS error and no 'Proceed' option, it has cached an HSTS policy from a previous certificate on this domain. Open the URL in a private/incognito window, or clear the HSTS entry at chrome://net-internals/#hsts (Delete domain).
tls.importDesc: Paste your own PEM certificate and private key.
tls.importPathDesc: Provide absolute paths to existing certificate and key files on disk (e.g. /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/*.pem). Files must be readable from inside the container.
tls.importPathHint: Files are symlinked, not copied. When the source files change on disk (e.g. after certbot renew), the server detects it within ~30s and restarts automatically to pick up the new certificate. The container has read-only access to the following host directories: /etc/letsencrypt (certbot), /root/cert (3x-ui), /root/.acme.sh (acme.sh).
tls.restarting: Certificate applied. Server is restarting with HTTPS...
tls.skipWarning: Without a TLS certificate the web interface is only accessible over HTTP.

login.rememberMeDesc: Stay logged after closing the browser

client.name: Name
client.expireDateDesc: Date the client will be disabled. Blank for permanent
client.deleteDialog1: Are you sure you want to delete
client.noPrivKey: This client has no known private key. Cannot create Configuration.
client.allowedIpsDesc: Which IPs will be routed through the VPN (overrides global config)
client.serverAllowedIpsDesc: Which IPs the server will route to the client
client.mtuDesc: Sets the maximum transmission unit (packet size) for the VPN tunnel
client.persistentKeepaliveDesc: Sets the interval (in seconds) for keep-alive packets. 0 disables it
client.hooksLeaveEmpty: Only for wg-quick. Otherwise, leave it empty
client.dnsDesc: DNS server clients will use (overrides global config)
client.endpointDesc: IP of the client from which the WireGuard connection is established
client.firewallIpsDesc: Destination IPs/CIDRs this client can access (server-side enforcement). Leave empty to use Allowed IPs. Supports optional port and protocol filtering. See docs for syntax.
client.presetDesc: Choose which obfuscator preset routes this client. Leave on default to follow the server's default preset.
client.nameHint: Latin letters, digits, hyphen, dot and {'@'} only; up to 32 characters; must be unique.

nameError.invalidChars: Only Latin letters, digits, hyphen, dot and {'@'} are allowed (no spaces or other special characters).
nameError.duplicate: A client with this name already exists.

toast.unknown: Unknown error. See console for more details

general.metricsPasswordDesc: Bearer password for the metrics endpoint. Leave blank to keep the current password.
general.metricsPasswordSet: Password is set — leave blank to keep it
general.jsonDesc: Route for metrics in JSON format

config.hostDesc: Public hostname or IP (invalidates client-related config)
config.allowedIpsDesc: Allowed IPs clients will use (global config)
config.dnsDesc: DNS server clients will use (global config)
config.mtuDesc: MTU clients will use (only for new clients)
config.persistentKeepaliveDesc: Interval in seconds to send keepalives to the server. 0 = disabled (only for new clients)

tls.title: TLS Certificate
tls.desc: Replace the active HTTPS certificate or switch to plain HTTP.
tls.applied: TLS configuration applied. Server is restarting.
tls.appliedRestart: TLS configuration applied. Server is restarting; reopen {url} in a few seconds.
tls.externalManaged: TLS is managed by an external reverse proxy (TLS_TERMINATION=external). The certificate must be issued and rotated outside of PhobosWG.

interface.deviceDesc: Ethernet device the wireguard traffic should be forwarded through
interface.restartWarn: Are you sure to restart the interface? This will disconnect all clients.
interface.ipv4CidrDesc: Address pool for clients (e.g. 10.8.0.0/24). Widen the mask (e.g. /16) to allow more clients or different ranges. Changing it reassigns every client's IPv4 address.
interface.ipv6CidrDesc: IPv6 address pool for clients. Changing it reassigns every client's IPv6 address.
interface.firewallEnabledDesc: Restrict client traffic to specific destination IPs using iptables. When enabled, each client can be configured with allowed destinations.
interface.publicIpV4Desc: Used as target in client obfuscator config.
interface.publicDomainDesc: When set, used as obfuscator target in client config instead of IPv4/IPv6.
interface.egressWarpDesc: When enabled, clients exit via Cloudflare WARP instead of the server's WAN, hiding the server IP. Requires a registered WARP configuration. A lower client MTU may be needed.

obfuscatorPresets.title: Obfuscator presets
obfuscatorPresets.desc: Each preset runs its own wg-obfuscator listener with independent port, key and masking. The default preset is used by every client that has no explicit assignment. Ports must be in the 51822-51921 range published by docker-compose.
obfuscatorPresets.addDesc: Enter a name only. Port, key and other parameters get sensible defaults and can be edited in the preset afterwards.
obfuscatorPresets.deleteConfirm: Delete this preset? Clients using it will fall back to the default preset.
obfuscatorPresets.nameDesc: Display name of the preset. Used only in the panel.
obfuscatorPresets.sourceLportDesc: Public UDP port the server obfuscator listens on. Must be within 51822-51921 — the range published by docker-compose, otherwise clients cannot reach it.
obfuscatorPresets.targetDesc: Where the server obfuscator forwards decapsulated traffic. Leave empty to use the local WireGuard endpoint (127.0.0.1:[wg-port]). Format: host:port.
obfuscatorPresets.sourceIfDesc: IP of the interface the server obfuscator binds to. Default 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces).
obfuscatorPresets.keyDesc: Shared obfuscation key, identical on both sides. Generated randomly (200-254 chars). 1-255 characters.
obfuscatorPresets.maskingDesc: DPI-evasion mode. STUN: wraps traffic as STUN. MEDIA: masks as RTP/H.264 media, set explicitly on both sides. AUTO: server auto-detects, client uses STUN. NONE: masking off (XOR stays).
obfuscatorPresets.obfuscateBytesDesc: How many leading bytes to XOR. 0 = whole packet. >0 disables dummy padding. Must match on both sides. For MEDIA: 0 auto-applies 16, minimum 4.
obfuscatorPresets.dummyDesc: Maximum random padding added per packet (0-1024). Default 40.
obfuscatorPresets.dummyDisabledNote: Not used with MEDIA or when obfuscate-bytes > 0.
obfuscatorPresets.verboseDesc: Log verbosity. error/warn/info/debug/trace. error is recommended in production; debug/trace heavily load the CPU.
obfuscatorPresets.clientLocalPortDesc: Local UDP port the client WireGuard sends to (Endpoint 127.0.0.1:[port]) and the client obfuscator's source-lport. Default 13255.

admin.introText:
Welcome to the admin panel.

Here you can manage general settings, configuration, the interface, obfuscator presets and the hooks.

Start by choosing one of the sections in the sidebar.

warp.title: Cloudflare WARP
warp.intro: Register a free Cloudflare WARP account to use it as the egress for your clients. Clients will exit with a Cloudflare IP instead of the server IP.
warp.notActiveEgress: WARP is not active as egress
warp.notActiveEgressTip: Enable "Route client traffic through WARP" in the Network interface section to use and test the WARP tunnel.
warp.licenseKeyDesc: Enter a WARP+ license key to enable WARP+.
warp.intervalDaysDesc: 0 to disable. Rotates the WARP IP automatically.
warp.importDesc: Paste a WireGuard config. The system will test the connection — if a handshake is established, the configuration will be saved. Table, PostUp and PostDown are managed automatically and should not be included.

generic.validNumberRange: {0} must be a valid number or number range

client.name: Name
client.firewallIpsInvalid: Invalid firewall IP entry. See docs for supported syntax.
client.nameTooLong: Client name must not exceed 32 characters.
client.nameInvalidChars: Client name may contain only Latin letters, digits, hyphen, dot and {'@'} (no spaces or other special characters).

obfuscatorPreset.name: Preset name
obfuscatorPreset.obfuscateBytesMedia: For MEDIA the minimum is 4 (or 0 for the default 16)

user.name: Name
user.emailInvalid: Email must be a valid email

interface.domainValid: Domain must be a valid hostname

zod.body: Body must be a valid object

copy.copyConfig: Click to copy config to clipboard
